content:
✨ GenRecon | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To reconstruct detailed and editable 3D meshes of indoor scenes from multiple 2D images by combining generative modeling with scene decomposition.
✨ Key Features: • High-fidelity 3D scene reconstruction from multi-view images • Integration of strong generative 3D priors (e.g., Trellis.2) • Scene reconstruction via spatially-localized, overlapping chunks
📖 Summary: GenRecon is a Python-based framework for high-fidelity 3D scene reconstruction from multi-view RGB images. It leverages strong generative 3D priors, specifically Trellis.2, by conditioning the generation process on localized scene chunks to achieve scene-scale reconstruction. The approach enables faithful, editable PBR mesh reconstructions of indoor environments, outperforming existing methods.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 torlink | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a fast, simple, and private way to find and download torrents directly from the command line without any complex setup.
✨ Key Features: • Terminal-based interface • Zero setup and configuration • Curated list of reputable torrent sources • Direct magnet link and infohash support • Background download management
📖 Summary: torlink is a terminal-based torrent finder and downloader that simplifies the process of locating and downloading torrent files. It searches a curated list of reputable sources, presents results clearly, and handles downloads directly within the terminal, offering a zero-setup, zero-configuration user experience.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ L0p4Map | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Network monitoring, visualization, and security analysis for security researchers.
✨ Key Features: • ARP Network Scan • Hostname Resolution • Device Fingerprinting • Role Detection • Real Network Topology Graph
📖 Summary: L0p4Map is a professional network monitoring and visualization tool designed for security researchers. It combines high-speed ARP scanning, hostname resolution, device fingerprinting, and role detection with a comprehensive nmap integration for deep network analysis and vulnerability assessment.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ codebase-memory-mcp | C
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide AI coding agents with fast, efficient, and comprehensive code intelligence by indexing codebases into a navigable knowledge graph.
✨ Key Features: • High-performance code indexing (milliseconds for average repo, minutes for Linux kernel) • Persistent knowledge graph creation
📖 Summary: Codebase-Memory-MCP is a high-performance C-based code intelligence engine that indexes entire codebases into a knowledge graph for AI coding agents. It achieves extreme indexing speeds and sub-millisecond query times, supporting 158 languages with enhanced semantic resolution for popular ones, and ships as a single, zero-dependency static binary.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 rlm | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a general plug-and-play inference library for Recursive Language Models (RLMs), enabling language models to process and interact with extremely long contexts by recursively calling themselves within a sandboxed environment.
✨ Key Features: • Task-agnostic inference paradigm for language models
📖 Summary: Recursive Language Models (RLMs) offer a novel inference paradigm for language models, enabling them to handle near-infinite context lengths by programmatically examining, decomposing, and recursively calling themselves. This library provides an extensible inference engine and training environment for RLMs, supporting various sandboxes and standard API-based or local LLMs.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 universal-android-debloater-next-generation | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: Removing unwanted system applications (bloatware) from Android devices to enhance privacy, security, and performance, without requiring root access.
✨ Key Features: • Cross-platform GUI application • Uses ADB for debloating • Supports non-rooted devices • Improves privacy, security, and battery life • Reduces attack surface by removing system apps
📖 Summary: Universal Android Debloater Next Generation (UAD-ng) is a cross-platform GUI application written in Rust that uses ADB to remove pre-installed system apps (bloatware) from non-rooted Android devices. It aims to improve device privacy, security, and performance by reducing the attack surface and freeing up resources.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
